jobs.title: Member Development Director
jobs.description:
Hello Everyone,

We are excited to announce that we are hiring for a Member Development Director role with one of our prestigious clients in the executive membership and business networking space.

Our client is looking for an experienced business development professional who is passionate about building relationships with founders, CEOs, and business leaders. This is an exciting opportunity to join the U.S. launch of a well-established international organization and play a key role in growing an exclusive community of entrepreneurs.

Position
: Member Development Director
Location
: New York, NY (Hybrid)

Key Responsibilit
ies:

• Identify, engage, and build relationships with founders, CEOs, and entrepreneurs.
• Manage a high-quality pipeline of prospective members through a consultative sales approach.
• Conduct discovery meetings and present the value of an exclusive executive membership.
• Partner with internal teams to ensure a seamless onboarding experience for new members.
• Represent the organization at networking events and contribute to the growth of a thriving founder community.

What We're Looking For:

• 6+ years of experience in Business Development, Executive Relationship Management, Membership Sales, or Consultative Sales.
• Experience engaging with C-suite executives, founders, or investors.
• Strong communication, relationship-building, and presentation skills.
• Existing network within the founder or executive ecosystem is a plus.

If you're interested or know someone who would be a great fit, please feel free to connect with me or send your resume directly to karishma.soni@meytier.com.

jobs.title: President and CEO
jobs.description:
The Wyckoff Family YMCA, located in northern Bergen County, is at the heart of the suburban Wyckoff community.

Job Description

Wyckoff Family YMCA

POSITION: President/CEO

Application Deadline
: July 24th

691 Wyckoff Avenue

Wyckoff, NJ 07481

www.wyckoffymca.org

Wyckoff Family YMCA is an equal opportunity employer. We work to ensure that everyone has the opportunity to reach their full potential with dignity.

THE OPPORTUNITY AT WYCKOFF FAMILY YMCA

The Wyckoff Family YMCA, located in northern Bergen County, is at the heart of the suburban Wyckoff community. It boasts a highly innovative program base, featuring two indoor pools, two full-sized gymnasiums, a performing arts center, and a newly renovated fitness center.

The outdoor campus comprises approximately 14 acres, including two park-like acres centered around a swimmable lake. In addition, the site provides access to two full-sized turf fields located adjacent to the campus. These fields are shared with the Town and local sports organizations and are not exclusively operated by the Y.

This Y is known for its high-level and robust involvement in community service and dedication to its philanthropic arm, Wyckoff Y Cares.

The President/CEO of the Wyckoff YMCA will work closely with the senior leadership team and Board of Directors to successfully advance the organization’s new strategic plan and continue building a clear vision for Youth Development, Healthy Living and Social Responsibility within the community it serves.

Qualifications

Criteria for Candidates:

Accredited 4-year college degree and or equivalent work experience.
Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven track record of budgetary and fiscal management more than $15M or more annually.
Minimum seven years prior experience in a senior management role with a YMCA or related experience at a comparable organization with direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Must be able to develop and strengthen community relationships.
Have the ability to develop innovative programs, review, and enhance programs being offered or considered for both economic and effectiveness in broadening our engagement in the communities in which we serve.
Strategic planning and Fundraising experience including annual, capital, securing grants and endowment experience.
YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to achieve within 3 years.

Essential Functions

KEY SKILLS AND ABILITIES OF OUR NEXT CEO

Role

The board believes its new CEO should be an experienced leader who has the background and qualities that will permit him or her to achieve excellence in the following roles:

Develop and Implement a Strategic Plan:
Work effectively with volunteers, community leaders, and key staff to create and develop a strategic plan that addresses the needs of the communities served by the Y and include defined organizational strategies, a clear implementation plan with deliverables, and measurable goals. Key Competencies: Change Leadership, Communication & Influence, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Program/Project Management

Improve Operating Performance:
Engage the board and appropriate staff to develop an annual tactical plan for identifying and achieving key goals and objectives for operating with safe, relevant, and sustainable practices. Quickly address issues that may arise, forecast future needs, and manage and direct the Y’s operations and activities. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Innovation

Fiscal Management:
Use high-level financial analysis to forecast financial trends, growth, and stability, while consistently leading the organization to balance or surplus operations by growing earned revenue and contributions and controlling costs. Empower and guide staff leaders to lead their departments to successful financial operations. Develop and implement stewardship strategies. Key Competencies: Fiscal Management, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Developing Self & Others, Philanthropy

Build and Lead Staff:
Ensure a talent development system is in place and executed effectively with cultural competence. Hire, motivate, nurture, retain, and hold staff accountable for key objectives. Work towards recruiting, retaining, developing, and promoting staff. Encourage the team by creating a spirit of camaraderie, connection, and sense of mission and purpose. Create a learning organization to help staff achieve their highest potential. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Inclusion, Emotional Maturity, Change Leadership

Position the Y as a Vital Partner for Community Change:
Serve as an ambassador for the mission, purpose, and vision for the YMCA within the community. Possess the ability to develop and cultivate relationships with key stakeholders from a variety of backgrounds, while always upholding the highest standards and values of the Y. Ensure your Y is seen as a leading nonprofit committed to strengthening community by connecting all people to their potential, purpose, and each other. Key Competencies: Communication & Influence, Engaging Community, Collaboration

Steward Fundraising Efforts:
Develop and implement an annual fundraising plan that will establish the YMCA as a leading nonprofit organization within the community through the engagement of members, volunteers, staff, and donors. Identify the need for successful capital fundraising, while taking the organization to a new level of effectiveness and impact in annual giving and endowment. Key Competencies: Philanthropy, Engaging Community, Communication & Influence

About The Wyckoff Family Ymca

Founded in 1944 as a summer camp for returning World War II veterans, the Wyckoff YMCA prides itself on serving all populations within the community.

The organization operates four robust full-day childcare centers; provides school-age childcare programming in over 21 schools across the county; welcomes 1,000 kids daily during summer camp season; spotlights a comprehensive performing arts initiative to include music, acting, and dance; and welcomes hundreds of special needs members of all ages to participate in all things “YMCA.”

Our Mission

The Wyckoff Family YMCA is a charitable Community Service Organization that is solely committed to meeting the challenges of the Communities that we serve. All are welcome to be part of the Y Community regardless of race, sex, faith, or financial position.

Our Impact

Youth Development – we empower young people to reach their full potential.
Healthy Living – we improve individual and community well-being.
Social Responsibility – we provide support and inspiring action in our communities.

Our Guiding Principles and Culture

The nearly 500 employees at the Wyckoff Y wear many hats and stand strong together, working side by side. Our friendly environment in the workplace is recognizable, and our members are closely aligned with staff.

Our culture is unique to us in that our employee retention rate has remained high through all the ups and downturns in the economy.

Current Key Strategic Initiatives

Preparing Youth for Success – advancing Wyckoff YMCA youth and teen initiatives by creating a new Teen Center.
Veterans Post – continue growing “The Patriot Club,” a space where service men and women gather together in camaraderie to take part in unique and innovative classes, interactive talks, and lectures.
Increasing Social Connections – further advancement of a robust Active Older Adult population through recreational and educational offerings.
Improving Community Health – working with Hackensack Meridan Health, our Mission Partner, to create workshops that promote healthy living, support the prevention and management of chronic disease, and empower people of all ages and abilities to lead healthier, more active lives.

Membership:
Total Number of Members: 13,000

Annual Support Campaign

2026 Goal: $250,000

2025 Total: $195,000

In addition to traditional annual giving campaigns, the Wyckoff YMCA runs special events that raise over $431,025 annually:

Annual Golf Outing Benefiting Special Needs Programs
Triathlon
Ladies Garden Party
Billy's Buddies Annual Walk
Mike O’Shea Charitable Golf Outing

Staff Team:
The Y’s workforce consists of approximately 460 employees (140 full-time), with an additional 250 seasonal employees in the summer.

Direct Reports To The President/CEO Include

COO
CFO
Director of Development Relations
Director of HR
Director of Education
Directors of Membership and Programming

Governance:
8 Board of Directors, with the following standing committees:

Finance
Membership
Facilities

Financial Snapshot

2026 Budget: $23 million
Long-Term Debt: $800,000 solar and roof project
Endowment: $13 million
2025 operating net: $75,000
2024 operating net: $103,300

Information Sites

YMCA Website: https://wyckoffymca.org/

Salary, Benefits, & Criteria

Salary Range:
$275,000-$325,000

Benefits

Health Insurance
YMCA Retirement (3% contributed by employee, 6% contributed by YMCA) once eligibility is met.
YMCA 403B savings account available
Generous Paid Time Off (PTO)
Complimentary YMCA Membership
Other benefits per personnel policy

Residency Expectation:
Living in the YMCA’s service area is required.

SEARCH PROCESS TARGET DATES

Resumes accepted through: July 24th
Preliminary interviews: September 8 & 9
Final interviews: September 29 & 30
New CEO on board (anticipated): January 1st, 2027
Dates subject to change

How To Apply

References, Background Check, and Organizational Leader Certification:

Candidates must submit (pdf format only) the following in their online application: a resume, cover letter. Applications submitted without a cover letter will not be considered.

Candidates who advance to final interviews will need to submit six references (four professional and two personal). References checked only with prior notification. Successful candidate accepting the offer must pass a background check and must have a current Y-USA’s Organizational Leader Certification or obtain certification within the first three years from start date as the CEO. The YMCA's personnel policies may require further screening.

Candidates Must Apply Via The Following Website

https://tinyurl.com/Wyckoff-Family-Y-CEO

QUESTIONS

About the Search Process?

Sandy Morander

Director CEO Search

[email protected]

210-284-5593

About the YMCA?

Paul Kieltyka

Vice President, Alliance Services

New Jersey YMCA Alliance

[email protected]

jobs.title: Senior Program Director
jobs.description:
YMCA Camp Bernie Senior Program Director

Job Description

The Senior Program Director provides senior-level leadership for YMCA Camp Bernie’s Outdoor Education programs, serving as the primary leader responsible for curriculum quality, instructional delivery, school partnerships, staff development, and participant experience. In addition to leading Outdoor Education, this position oversees summer camp operations, year-round programs, groups, retreats, and related camp experiences to ensure consistent quality, safety, mission alignment, and operational excellence across all program areas. Reporting directly to the SVP of Childcare and Camps and serving as second in command, this role serves as a trusted strategic and operational partner, supporting executive decision-making, departmental planning, budget management, staff leadership, risk management, and cross-functional coordination across camp operations.

Qualifications

Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Outdoor Education, Recreation Management, Nonprofit Management, Business Administration, or a related field required.
Experience: Minimum of 5 years of progressively responsible experience in outdoor education, camp leadership, youth development, residential camp operations, nonprofit programming, or a related field, including at least 2 years of supervisory or team leadership experience.
Preferred experience: Experience overseeing Outdoor Education programs and supporting summer camp or year-round camp operations in a YMCA, residential camp, retreat center, school partnership, or similar environment.
Leadership Skills: Demonstrated ability to lead teams, manage complex camp operations, support executive-level priorities, build school and community partnerships, make sound decisions, communicate effectively, and deliver high-quality service in a mission-driven environment. CPR/AED/First Aid certification or willingness to obtain required, along with mandatory state background checks and clearances.
Preferred Certifications: ACCT Level 2 or CCM and/or American Red Cross lifeguard or Lifeguard Instructor.
Strategic Thinking and Innovation: Ability to identify opportunities, improve systems, solve problems, and develop creative program and business strategies that advance community impact and financial sustainability.
Technical Proficiency: Strong operational knowledge of Microsoft Office suite and ability to use registration, scheduling, reporting, and communication systems to manage departmental performance.
Physical Stamina: Ability to perform indoor and outdoor activities, ascend/descend stairs, lift to 50 lbs., walk on uneven surfaces, and operate office machinery.
Flexible Schedule: Ability to work off-hours and weekends for special events when required.

Essential Functions

Outdoor Education Leadership: Lead the vision, planning, delivery, and continuous improvement of Camp Bernie’s Outdoor Education programs as the primary focus of the position. Develop and refine curriculum, instructional standards, schedules, staff training, and program systems that support high-quality school, youth, and community experiences. Ensure Outdoor Education programs are mission-centered, age-appropriate, engaging, safe, and aligned with YMCA values and educational outcomes.
Summer Camp and Program Oversight: Provide senior oversight for summer camp, year-round camp programs, groups, retreats, and special program experiences. Partner with program leaders to ensure consistent standards for staffing, supervision, schedules, participant care, program quality, and family or client communication. Support planning and execution across seasons to strengthen program continuity, camper and participant retention, and overall camp impact.
Senior Operational Leadership: Serve as the number two operational leader to the SVP of Childcare and Camps for YMCA Camp Bernie. Support executive leadership with day-to-day decision-making, annual planning, budget development, revenue and expense monitoring, staff structure, compliance, risk management, and implementation of strategic priorities. Provide leadership coverage and direction when the SVP is unavailable or when delegated.
Staff Leadership and Cross-Functional Coordination: Recruit, hire, train, supervise, coach, and evaluate Outdoor Education and program staff while supporting seasonal leadership teams. Coordinate closely with facilities, food service, registration, marketing, finance, and other YMCA departments to ensure programs are well-planned, adequately staffed, properly resourced, and delivered with a high level of service and professionalism.
Act as the SVP’s senior program and operations partner, representing camp leadership in meetings, supporting enterprise-wide initiatives, preparing reports and recommendations, leading special projects, and helping translate strategic priorities into consistent execution across Outdoor Education, summer camp, retreats, groups, and year-round programs.
Perform all other duties as assigned.

Benefits

Health, vision, and dental insurance
Paid vacation, sick, and bereavement days
Retirement Savings & 403(b) Smart Account after eligibility requirements are met.
On-Site Housing

jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
About Dog Tag

Dog Tag is a nonprofit social enterprise that combines our one-of-a-kind Fellowship program, which serves veterans with service-connected disabilities, military spouses, and caregivers nationwide, with a high-quality bakery. At the bakery, our community can express support for our mission through a cup of coffee and a variety of baked products, sandwiches, and beverages. Based in Washington, DC’s Georgetown neighborhood, Dog Tag provides an innovative, hands-on business education program that equips Fellows with the skills to launch their own businesses, transition to meaningful careers in the civilian workforce, or continue their educational priorities.

The bakery is where delicious treats and meaningful service meet. Every item we create reflects our dedication to supporting those who have served our country, and every sale enables us to serve more Fellows and alumni. The ability to engage with us in our cafe, as well as through local catering and gift boxes shipped nationwide, extends the reach of our mission to every service member family seeking renewed purpose, identity, and commitment.

Why Dog Tag?

Our values of service, entrepreneurship, empowerment, and respect guide everything we do — from how we interact with our team to how we engage with our customers. As part of our growing team, you’ll have the opportunity to contribute to a high-impact mission that extends beyond a storefront. You’ll help empower our Fellows and alumni and make an impact every day.

Working at Dog Tag is more than a job; everyone on staff takes part in the Fellowship Program, plays a role in telling the story of Dog Tag's impact to customers, partners, donors, and neighbors, and becomes an advocate for the mission.

Dog Tag is a workplace for those who want to invest in themselves and others. We value dedication and high-quality outcomes for all staff members because every individual contributes to the success of our entire community.

Role Description

The Chief Development Officer (CDO) is an integral member of the Dog Tag leadership team and is responsible for inspiring the development team to deliver our fundraising goals. This leader works collaboratively with the marketing, programs, bakery, and operations teams to communicate Dog Tag’s unique values that align with donors’ priorities and missions.

The CDO manages direct reports including a Gifts Officer and a Development Coordinator. Working as a unified team, these high-impact thought partners are responsible for securing philanthropic gifts from mission-aligned organizations and individuals to fuel Dog Tag’s mission. The CDO will partner with the CEO and leadership team colleagues to ensure our systems, communications, and reporting cadences align with donors’ and program participants’ expectations. As Dog Tag’s key fundraising steward, the CDO will think proactively and act creatively to anticipate the unknown to champion the organization’s expansion-focused strategies.

Importantly, the CDO will also embody the One Dog Tag Culture through all actions, communications, and leadership engagements. This leader will ensure that all members of the Dog Tag team, both employees and stakeholders, are treated with dignity and respect, building a brand and a unified spirit to serve program participants and inspire partners to drive impact that exceeds expectations.

Working at Dog Tag is more than a job; everyone on staff takes part in the Fellowship Program, plays a role in telling the story of Dog Tag's impact to customers, partners, donors, and neighbors, and becomes an advocate for the mission. Dog Tag is a workplace for those who want to invest in themselves and others. We value dedication and high-quality outcomes for all staff members because every individual contributes to the success of our entire community.

Responsibilities & Duties

Leadership and Development Strategy:

Develop, manage, and execute Dog Tag’s development and stewardship plans to achieve annual revenue goals.
Update existing development and stewardship plans annually with new ideas, industry best practices, and data-driven analysis.
Ensure Dog Tag’s development plan aligns with and is in service of the evolving organizational goals.
In partnership with the CEO and COO, set and exceed annual revenue goals.
Ensure a robust gift pipeline big enough to hit annual revenue goals by actively managing prospective and current corporations, foundations, and individual donors.
Project manage the CEO’s fundraising work, directing where and how best to spend time and efforts on a weekly, monthly, and quarterly basis.
Cultivate relationships with all members of the board of directors to support their strategic development efforts, provide training, and facilitate the process to develop potential funder parameters.
Monitor and report regularly on the progress of the development plan, providing actionable analysis for the CEO and board of directors.
Identify suspected revenue gaps and develop tactics to generate new leads.
Contribute positively to Dog Tag’s organizational culture and lead with Dog Tag’s values.

Corporate and Foundation Fundraising

Collaborate with CEO and Dog Tag’s board of directors to codify and implement a corporate partnership model that aligns with the organization’s values.
Build personal relationships with foundations and corporate funders. Research, design, and lead moves management plans.
Ensure Dog Tag has a robust pipeline of local, regional, and national grant opportunities to pursue.
Working with the part-time grant writer, Gifts Officer, and Development Coordinator, create and manage a grant calendar to track all upcoming applications and reports.
Clearly and effectively draft dynamic, reader-friendly, and high-quality writing that is free of typos, jargon, and lackluster prose.
Collaborate across teams to gather materials and requirements for grant applications and reports.

Individual Giving

Research, design, and lead the moves management plans for major individual donors. Manage a portfolio of major donors.
Oversee the management and development of existing initiatives, appeals, and campaigns focused on targeting new and stewarding existing individual donors.

Development Communications

Help develop creative and mission-focused fundraising messaging that effectively articulates the special role and impact of Dog Tag in the community.
Partner with program team to effectively share their expertise, vision, and impact with donors. Inspire the creation of new programs that expand the impact of Dog Tag’s mission and align with corporate, foundation, and individual priorities.
Collaborate with the communications staff to update the communications plan/calendar to support development goals, and work together to ensure opportunities for both programs and development are elevated.

Development Operations and Revenue Tracking

Establish and monitor annual and long-term revenue goals and budgets.
Design and update a regular progress-to-goal reports and development dashboard for the CEO and the board.
Spearhead development-focused data input and ensure accuracy in all records and development reports in Dog Tag’s CRM system (Salesforce).
Work closely with COO to ensure data integrity.
Work closely with the Finance team to support management of restricted funds and donor-required reporting.

Education and Experience

A minimum of 7 years of nonprofit development experience.
A minimum of 3 years of nonprofit leadership experience.
Demonstrable experience with building successful corporate partnerships, foundation relations, and individual major gift relationships.
In-depth experience working in close partnership with senior management and board members on major philanthropic initiatives and campaigns.
Enthusiasm to propose fresh ideas without prompting by leadership, openness to feedback on those ideas, and a persistence to bring those ideas to life.
An excellent working knowledge of CRM systems for donor and revenue management.
Experience with and knowledge of the Washington, DC funder community is preferred.

Work Hours, Compensation & Benefits

This is a full-time, hybrid position based in Washington, DC.
Starting salary: $150,000 annually (based on experience) and a competitive performance-based incentive plan.
Paid leave, including vacation and sick leave and holidays.
Paid end-of-year closing: in celebration of the incredible work done across all departments during the year, Dog Tag closes between Christmas and New Year.
Medical (health, dental, and vision) insurance, life & accident insurance, and Employee Assistance Program.
Voluntary benefits include flexible spending accounts (medical, dependent care, transportation), short-term and long-term disability insurance, and retirement plans.
Free shift drinks and food discounts.
Opportunities for training and career growth.

jobs.title: Development Director - More Perfect Union
jobs.description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Development Director - More Perfect Union based in the United States.

This role offers the opportunity to lead fundraising strategy for a rapidly growing mission-driven media and advocacy organization.

The Development Director will play a key role in expanding major gift programs, strengthening donor relationships, and building long-term financial sustainability.

Working closely with executive leadership, this position will shape fundraising priorities and develop strategies that support organizational growth.

The ideal candidate will combine strategic thinking, strong relationship-building abilities, and exceptional storytelling skills.

This position requires a collaborative professional who can create compelling donor materials and manage complex development initiatives.

With ownership of major donor engagement and fundraising systems, the role provides meaningful influence over the future direction of the organization.

This is an opportunity for an experienced nonprofit fundraising leader passionate about advancing impactful social change.

Accountabilities

The Development Director will lead the organization’s fundraising efforts by developing major gift strategies, cultivating donor relationships, and creating systems that support sustainable growth. This role requires close collaboration with senior leadership, strong project management skills, and the ability to translate organizational impact into compelling fundraising narratives.

Develop and execute major gift fundraising strategies in partnership with executive leadership, including donor prioritization, portfolio management, and engagement plans.
Manage relationships with major donors through written communications, phone calls, meetings, stewardship activities, and ongoing engagement.
Conduct prospect research and identify new opportunities for donor and foundation support.
Lead preparation of donor materials, including pitch decks, proposals, grant narratives, donor briefs, and organizational background documents.
Support fundraising outreach efforts through research, meeting preparation, follow-ups, and relationship tracking.
Create innovative donor engagement strategies, solicitation approaches, and stewardship initiatives to expand financial support.
Advise senior leaders on fundraising goals, development priorities, projections, and relationship management strategies.
Monitor fundraising activity, track progress toward goals, and identify opportunities to improve development performance.
Maintain accurate development records and support CRM management processes.
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure fundraising materials effectively communicate organizational priorities, impact, and future plans.
Manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining high-quality work in a fast-paced environment.
Participate in donor meetings and travel as needed.

Requirements

The ideal candidate will bring significant nonprofit fundraising experience, strong communication abilities, and a passion for mission-driven work. This position requires someone who can independently manage development initiatives while collaborating effectively with senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

5+ years of experience in nonprofit, political fundraising, advocacy, or media development roles.
At least 3 years of experience directly soliciting major gifts.
Proven experience managing major donor relationships and foundation fundraising initiatives.
Experience creating donor-facing materials such as proposals, presentations, decks, and strategic communications.
Experience supporting principal-level donor management and high-level fundraising efforts.
Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills with the ability to communicate organizational impact effectively.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities, deadlines, and projects.
Familiarity with Salesforce or similar CRM platforms.
Ability to work closely with executive leadership and provide strategic fundraising support.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment requiring flexibility and initiative.
Ability to build systems, processes, and workflows within a growing organization.
Strong alignment with the organization’s mission and commitment to equity, inclusion, and social impact.
Willingness and ability to travel, including occasional evening or weekend commitments.

Benefits

Competitive full-time salary of approximately $150,000 annually.
Fully remote work environment.
Opportunity to lead fundraising strategy for a growing mission-driven organization.
High level of ownership and influence over development initiatives.
Collaborative work environment with senior leadership and cross-functional teams.
Opportunity to build and improve fundraising systems and processes.
Meaningful work supporting advocacy, journalism, and education initiatives.
Professional growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding organization.

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process
to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice:
By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

new_jobs.title: President and CEO
new_jobs.description:
The Wyckoff Family YMCA, located in northern Bergen County, is at the heart of the suburban Wyckoff community.

Job Description

Wyckoff Family YMCA

POSITION: President/CEO

Application Deadline
: July 24th

691 Wyckoff Avenue

Wyckoff, NJ 07481

www.wyckoffymca.org

Wyckoff Family YMCA is an equal opportunity employer. We work to ensure that everyone has the opportunity to reach their full potential with dignity.

THE OPPORTUNITY AT WYCKOFF FAMILY YMCA

The Wyckoff Family YMCA, located in northern Bergen County, is at the heart of the suburban Wyckoff community. It boasts a highly innovative program base, featuring two indoor pools, two full-sized gymnasiums, a performing arts center, and a newly renovated fitness center.

The outdoor campus comprises approximately 14 acres, including two park-like acres centered around a swimmable lake. In addition, the site provides access to two full-sized turf fields located adjacent to the campus. These fields are shared with the Town and local sports organizations and are not exclusively operated by the Y.

This Y is known for its high-level and robust involvement in community service and dedication to its philanthropic arm, Wyckoff Y Cares.

The President/CEO of the Wyckoff YMCA will work closely with the senior leadership team and Board of Directors to successfully advance the organization’s new strategic plan and continue building a clear vision for Youth Development, Healthy Living and Social Responsibility within the community it serves.

Qualifications

Criteria for Candidates:

Accredited 4-year college degree and or equivalent work experience.
Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven track record of budgetary and fiscal management more than $15M or more annually.
Minimum seven years prior experience in a senior management role with a YMCA or related experience at a comparable organization with direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Must be able to develop and strengthen community relationships.
Have the ability to develop innovative programs, review, and enhance programs being offered or considered for both economic and effectiveness in broadening our engagement in the communities in which we serve.
Strategic planning and Fundraising experience including annual, capital, securing grants and endowment experience.
YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to achieve within 3 years.

Essential Functions

KEY SKILLS AND ABILITIES OF OUR NEXT CEO

Role

The board believes its new CEO should be an experienced leader who has the background and qualities that will permit him or her to achieve excellence in the following roles:

Develop and Implement a Strategic Plan:
Work effectively with volunteers, community leaders, and key staff to create and develop a strategic plan that addresses the needs of the communities served by the Y and include defined organizational strategies, a clear implementation plan with deliverables, and measurable goals. Key Competencies: Change Leadership, Communication & Influence, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Program/Project Management

Improve Operating Performance:
Engage the board and appropriate staff to develop an annual tactical plan for identifying and achieving key goals and objectives for operating with safe, relevant, and sustainable practices. Quickly address issues that may arise, forecast future needs, and manage and direct the Y’s operations and activities. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Innovation

Fiscal Management:
Use high-level financial analysis to forecast financial trends, growth, and stability, while consistently leading the organization to balance or surplus operations by growing earned revenue and contributions and controlling costs. Empower and guide staff leaders to lead their departments to successful financial operations. Develop and implement stewardship strategies. Key Competencies: Fiscal Management, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Developing Self & Others, Philanthropy

Build and Lead Staff:
Ensure a talent development system is in place and executed effectively with cultural competence. Hire, motivate, nurture, retain, and hold staff accountable for key objectives. Work towards recruiting, retaining, developing, and promoting staff. Encourage the team by creating a spirit of camaraderie, connection, and sense of mission and purpose. Create a learning organization to help staff achieve their highest potential. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Inclusion, Emotional Maturity, Change Leadership

Position the Y as a Vital Partner for Community Change:
Serve as an ambassador for the mission, purpose, and vision for the YMCA within the community. Possess the ability to develop and cultivate relationships with key stakeholders from a variety of backgrounds, while always upholding the highest standards and values of the Y. Ensure your Y is seen as a leading nonprofit committed to strengthening community by connecting all people to their potential, purpose, and each other. Key Competencies: Communication & Influence, Engaging Community, Collaboration

Steward Fundraising Efforts:
Develop and implement an annual fundraising plan that will establish the YMCA as a leading nonprofit organization within the community through the engagement of members, volunteers, staff, and donors. Identify the need for successful capital fundraising, while taking the organization to a new level of effectiveness and impact in annual giving and endowment. Key Competencies: Philanthropy, Engaging Community, Communication & Influence

About The Wyckoff Family Ymca

Founded in 1944 as a summer camp for returning World War II veterans, the Wyckoff YMCA prides itself on serving all populations within the community.

The organization operates four robust full-day childcare centers; provides school-age childcare programming in over 21 schools across the county; welcomes 1,000 kids daily during summer camp season; spotlights a comprehensive performing arts initiative to include music, acting, and dance; and welcomes hundreds of special needs members of all ages to participate in all things “YMCA.”

Our Mission

The Wyckoff Family YMCA is a charitable Community Service Organization that is solely committed to meeting the challenges of the Communities that we serve. All are welcome to be part of the Y Community regardless of race, sex, faith, or financial position.

Our Impact

Youth Development – we empower young people to reach their full potential.
Healthy Living – we improve individual and community well-being.
Social Responsibility – we provide support and inspiring action in our communities.

Our Guiding Principles and Culture

The nearly 500 employees at the Wyckoff Y wear many hats and stand strong together, working side by side. Our friendly environment in the workplace is recognizable, and our members are closely aligned with staff.

Our culture is unique to us in that our employee retention rate has remained high through all the ups and downturns in the economy.

Current Key Strategic Initiatives

Preparing Youth for Success – advancing Wyckoff YMCA youth and teen initiatives by creating a new Teen Center.
Veterans Post – continue growing “The Patriot Club,” a space where service men and women gather together in camaraderie to take part in unique and innovative classes, interactive talks, and lectures.
Increasing Social Connections – further advancement of a robust Active Older Adult population through recreational and educational offerings.
Improving Community Health – working with Hackensack Meridan Health, our Mission Partner, to create workshops that promote healthy living, support the prevention and management of chronic disease, and empower people of all ages and abilities to lead healthier, more active lives.

Membership:
Total Number of Members: 13,000

Annual Support Campaign

2026 Goal: $250,000

2025 Total: $195,000

In addition to traditional annual giving campaigns, the Wyckoff YMCA runs special events that raise over $431,025 annually:

Annual Golf Outing Benefiting Special Needs Programs
Triathlon
Ladies Garden Party
Billy's Buddies Annual Walk
Mike O’Shea Charitable Golf Outing

Staff Team:
The Y’s workforce consists of approximately 460 employees (140 full-time), with an additional 250 seasonal employees in the summer.

Direct Reports To The President/CEO Include

COO
CFO
Director of Development Relations
Director of HR
Director of Education
Directors of Membership and Programming

Governance:
8 Board of Directors, with the following standing committees:

Finance
Membership
Facilities

Financial Snapshot

2026 Budget: $23 million
Long-Term Debt: $800,000 solar and roof project
Endowment: $13 million
2025 operating net: $75,000
2024 operating net: $103,300

Information Sites

YMCA Website: https://wyckoffymca.org/

Salary, Benefits, & Criteria

Salary Range:
$275,000-$325,000

Benefits

Health Insurance
YMCA Retirement (3% contributed by employee, 6% contributed by YMCA) once eligibility is met.
YMCA 403B savings account available
Generous Paid Time Off (PTO)
Complimentary YMCA Membership
Other benefits per personnel policy

Residency Expectation:
Living in the YMCA’s service area is required.

SEARCH PROCESS TARGET DATES

Resumes accepted through: July 24th
Preliminary interviews: September 8 & 9
Final interviews: September 29 & 30
New CEO on board (anticipated): January 1st, 2027
Dates subject to change

How To Apply

References, Background Check, and Organizational Leader Certification:

Candidates must submit (pdf format only) the following in their online application: a resume, cover letter. Applications submitted without a cover letter will not be considered.

Candidates who advance to final interviews will need to submit six references (four professional and two personal). References checked only with prior notification. Successful candidate accepting the offer must pass a background check and must have a current Y-USA’s Organizational Leader Certification or obtain certification within the first three years from start date as the CEO. The YMCA's personnel policies may require further screening.

Candidates Must Apply Via The Following Website

https://tinyurl.com/Wyckoff-Family-Y-CEO

QUESTIONS

new_jobs.title: Senior Program Director
new_jobs.description:
YMCA Camp Bernie Senior Program Director

Job Description

The Senior Program Director provides senior-level leadership for YMCA Camp Bernie’s Outdoor Education programs, serving as the primary leader responsible for curriculum quality, instructional delivery, school partnerships, staff development, and participant experience. In addition to leading Outdoor Education, this position oversees summer camp operations, year-round programs, groups, retreats, and related camp experiences to ensure consistent quality, safety, mission alignment, and operational excellence across all program areas. Reporting directly to the SVP of Childcare and Camps and serving as second in command, this role serves as a trusted strategic and operational partner, supporting executive decision-making, departmental planning, budget management, staff leadership, risk management, and cross-functional coordination across camp operations.

Qualifications

Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Outdoor Education, Recreation Management, Nonprofit Management, Business Administration, or a related field required.
Experience: Minimum of 5 years of progressively responsible experience in outdoor education, camp leadership, youth development, residential camp operations, nonprofit programming, or a related field, including at least 2 years of supervisory or team leadership experience.
Preferred experience: Experience overseeing Outdoor Education programs and supporting summer camp or year-round camp operations in a YMCA, residential camp, retreat center, school partnership, or similar environment.
Leadership Skills: Demonstrated ability to lead teams, manage complex camp operations, support executive-level priorities, build school and community partnerships, make sound decisions, communicate effectively, and deliver high-quality service in a mission-driven environment. CPR/AED/First Aid certification or willingness to obtain required, along with mandatory state background checks and clearances.
Preferred Certifications: ACCT Level 2 or CCM and/or American Red Cross lifeguard or Lifeguard Instructor.
Strategic Thinking and Innovation: Ability to identify opportunities, improve systems, solve problems, and develop creative program and business strategies that advance community impact and financial sustainability.
Technical Proficiency: Strong operational knowledge of Microsoft Office suite and ability to use registration, scheduling, reporting, and communication systems to manage departmental performance.
Physical Stamina: Ability to perform indoor and outdoor activities, ascend/descend stairs, lift to 50 lbs., walk on uneven surfaces, and operate office machinery.
Flexible Schedule: Ability to work off-hours and weekends for special events when required.

Essential Functions

Outdoor Education Leadership: Lead the vision, planning, delivery, and continuous improvement of Camp Bernie’s Outdoor Education programs as the primary focus of the position. Develop and refine curriculum, instructional standards, schedules, staff training, and program systems that support high-quality school, youth, and community experiences. Ensure Outdoor Education programs are mission-centered, age-appropriate, engaging, safe, and aligned with YMCA values and educational outcomes.
Summer Camp and Program Oversight: Provide senior oversight for summer camp, year-round camp programs, groups, retreats, and special program experiences. Partner with program leaders to ensure consistent standards for staffing, supervision, schedules, participant care, program quality, and family or client communication. Support planning and execution across seasons to strengthen program continuity, camper and participant retention, and overall camp impact.
Senior Operational Leadership: Serve as the number two operational leader to the SVP of Childcare and Camps for YMCA Camp Bernie. Support executive leadership with day-to-day decision-making, annual planning, budget development, revenue and expense monitoring, staff structure, compliance, risk management, and implementation of strategic priorities. Provide leadership coverage and direction when the SVP is unavailable or when delegated.
Staff Leadership and Cross-Functional Coordination: Recruit, hire, train, supervise, coach, and evaluate Outdoor Education and program staff while supporting seasonal leadership teams. Coordinate closely with facilities, food service, registration, marketing, finance, and other YMCA departments to ensure programs are well-planned, adequately staffed, properly resourced, and delivered with a high level of service and professionalism.
Act as the SVP’s senior program and operations partner, representing camp leadership in meetings, supporting enterprise-wide initiatives, preparing reports and recommendations, leading special projects, and helping translate strategic priorities into consistent execution across Outdoor Education, summer camp, retreats, groups, and year-round programs.
Perform all other duties as assigned.

Benefits

Health, vision, and dental insurance
Paid vacation, sick, and bereavement days
Retirement Savings & 403(b) Smart Account after eligibility requirements are met.
On-Site Housing

new_jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
new_jobs.description:
About Dog Tag

Dog Tag is a nonprofit social enterprise that combines our one-of-a-kind Fellowship program, which serves veterans with service-connected disabilities, military spouses, and caregivers nationwide, with a high-quality bakery. At the bakery, our community can express support for our mission through a cup of coffee and a variety of baked products, sandwiches, and beverages. Based in Washington, DC’s Georgetown neighborhood, Dog Tag provides an innovative, hands-on business education program that equips Fellows with the skills to launch their own businesses, transition to meaningful careers in the civilian workforce, or continue their educational priorities.

The bakery is where delicious treats and meaningful service meet. Every item we create reflects our dedication to supporting those who have served our country, and every sale enables us to serve more Fellows and alumni. The ability to engage with us in our cafe, as well as through local catering and gift boxes shipped nationwide, extends the reach of our mission to every service member family seeking renewed purpose, identity, and commitment.

Why Dog Tag?

Our values of service, entrepreneurship, empowerment, and respect guide everything we do — from how we interact with our team to how we engage with our customers. As part of our growing team, you’ll have the opportunity to contribute to a high-impact mission that extends beyond a storefront. You’ll help empower our Fellows and alumni and make an impact every day.

Working at Dog Tag is more than a job; everyone on staff takes part in the Fellowship Program, plays a role in telling the story of Dog Tag's impact to customers, partners, donors, and neighbors, and becomes an advocate for the mission.

Dog Tag is a workplace for those who want to invest in themselves and others. We value dedication and high-quality outcomes for all staff members because every individual contributes to the success of our entire community.

Role Description

The Chief Development Officer (CDO) is an integral member of the Dog Tag leadership team and is responsible for inspiring the development team to deliver our fundraising goals. This leader works collaboratively with the marketing, programs, bakery, and operations teams to communicate Dog Tag’s unique values that align with donors’ priorities and missions.

The CDO manages direct reports including a Gifts Officer and a Development Coordinator. Working as a unified team, these high-impact thought partners are responsible for securing philanthropic gifts from mission-aligned organizations and individuals to fuel Dog Tag’s mission. The CDO will partner with the CEO and leadership team colleagues to ensure our systems, communications, and reporting cadences align with donors’ and program participants’ expectations. As Dog Tag’s key fundraising steward, the CDO will think proactively and act creatively to anticipate the unknown to champion the organization’s expansion-focused strategies.

Importantly, the CDO will also embody the One Dog Tag Culture through all actions, communications, and leadership engagements. This leader will ensure that all members of the Dog Tag team, both employees and stakeholders, are treated with dignity and respect, building a brand and a unified spirit to serve program participants and inspire partners to drive impact that exceeds expectations.

Working at Dog Tag is more than a job; everyone on staff takes part in the Fellowship Program, plays a role in telling the story of Dog Tag's impact to customers, partners, donors, and neighbors, and becomes an advocate for the mission. Dog Tag is a workplace for those who want to invest in themselves and others. We value dedication and high-quality outcomes for all staff members because every individual contributes to the success of our entire community.

Responsibilities & Duties

Leadership and Development Strategy:

Develop, manage, and execute Dog Tag’s development and stewardship plans to achieve annual revenue goals.
Update existing development and stewardship plans annually with new ideas, industry best practices, and data-driven analysis.
Ensure Dog Tag’s development plan aligns with and is in service of the evolving organizational goals.
In partnership with the CEO and COO, set and exceed annual revenue goals.
Ensure a robust gift pipeline big enough to hit annual revenue goals by actively managing prospective and current corporations, foundations, and individual donors.
Project manage the CEO’s fundraising work, directing where and how best to spend time and efforts on a weekly, monthly, and quarterly basis.
Cultivate relationships with all members of the board of directors to support their strategic development efforts, provide training, and facilitate the process to develop potential funder parameters.
Monitor and report regularly on the progress of the development plan, providing actionable analysis for the CEO and board of directors.
Identify suspected revenue gaps and develop tactics to generate new leads.
Contribute positively to Dog Tag’s organizational culture and lead with Dog Tag’s values.

Corporate and Foundation Fundraising

Collaborate with CEO and Dog Tag’s board of directors to codify and implement a corporate partnership model that aligns with the organization’s values.
Build personal relationships with foundations and corporate funders. Research, design, and lead moves management plans.
Ensure Dog Tag has a robust pipeline of local, regional, and national grant opportunities to pursue.
Working with the part-time grant writer, Gifts Officer, and Development Coordinator, create and manage a grant calendar to track all upcoming applications and reports.
Clearly and effectively draft dynamic, reader-friendly, and high-quality writing that is free of typos, jargon, and lackluster prose.
Collaborate across teams to gather materials and requirements for grant applications and reports.

Individual Giving

Research, design, and lead the moves management plans for major individual donors. Manage a portfolio of major donors.
Oversee the management and development of existing initiatives, appeals, and campaigns focused on targeting new and stewarding existing individual donors.

Development Communications

Help develop creative and mission-focused fundraising messaging that effectively articulates the special role and impact of Dog Tag in the community.
Partner with program team to effectively share their expertise, vision, and impact with donors. Inspire the creation of new programs that expand the impact of Dog Tag’s mission and align with corporate, foundation, and individual priorities.
Collaborate with the communications staff to update the communications plan/calendar to support development goals, and work together to ensure opportunities for both programs and development are elevated.

Development Operations and Revenue Tracking

Establish and monitor annual and long-term revenue goals and budgets.
Design and update a regular progress-to-goal reports and development dashboard for the CEO and the board.
Spearhead development-focused data input and ensure accuracy in all records and development reports in Dog Tag’s CRM system (Salesforce).
Work closely with COO to ensure data integrity.
Work closely with the Finance team to support management of restricted funds and donor-required reporting.

Education and Experience

A minimum of 7 years of nonprofit development experience.
A minimum of 3 years of nonprofit leadership experience.
Demonstrable experience with building successful corporate partnerships, foundation relations, and individual major gift relationships.
In-depth experience working in close partnership with senior management and board members on major philanthropic initiatives and campaigns.
Enthusiasm to propose fresh ideas without prompting by leadership, openness to feedback on those ideas, and a persistence to bring those ideas to life.
An excellent working knowledge of CRM systems for donor and revenue management.
Experience with and knowledge of the Washington, DC funder community is preferred.

Work Hours, Compensation & Benefits

This is a full-time, hybrid position based in Washington, DC.
Starting salary: $150,000 annually (based on experience) and a competitive performance-based incentive plan.
Paid leave, including vacation and sick leave and holidays.
Paid end-of-year closing: in celebration of the incredible work done across all departments during the year, Dog Tag closes between Christmas and New Year.
Medical (health, dental, and vision) insurance, life & accident insurance, and Employee Assistance Program.
Voluntary benefits include flexible spending accounts (medical, dependent care, transportation), short-term and long-term disability insurance, and retirement plans.
Free shift drinks and food discounts.
Opportunities for training and career growth.

new_jobs.title: Development Director - More Perfect Union
new_jobs.description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Development Director - More Perfect Union based in the United States.

This role offers the opportunity to lead fundraising strategy for a rapidly growing mission-driven media and advocacy organization.

The Development Director will play a key role in expanding major gift programs, strengthening donor relationships, and building long-term financial sustainability.

Working closely with executive leadership, this position will shape fundraising priorities and develop strategies that support organizational growth.

The ideal candidate will combine strategic thinking, strong relationship-building abilities, and exceptional storytelling skills.

This position requires a collaborative professional who can create compelling donor materials and manage complex development initiatives.

With ownership of major donor engagement and fundraising systems, the role provides meaningful influence over the future direction of the organization.

This is an opportunity for an experienced nonprofit fundraising leader passionate about advancing impactful social change.

Accountabilities

The Development Director will lead the organization’s fundraising efforts by developing major gift strategies, cultivating donor relationships, and creating systems that support sustainable growth. This role requires close collaboration with senior leadership, strong project management skills, and the ability to translate organizational impact into compelling fundraising narratives.

Develop and execute major gift fundraising strategies in partnership with executive leadership, including donor prioritization, portfolio management, and engagement plans.
Manage relationships with major donors through written communications, phone calls, meetings, stewardship activities, and ongoing engagement.
Conduct prospect research and identify new opportunities for donor and foundation support.
Lead preparation of donor materials, including pitch decks, proposals, grant narratives, donor briefs, and organizational background documents.
Support fundraising outreach efforts through research, meeting preparation, follow-ups, and relationship tracking.
Create innovative donor engagement strategies, solicitation approaches, and stewardship initiatives to expand financial support.
Advise senior leaders on fundraising goals, development priorities, projections, and relationship management strategies.
Monitor fundraising activity, track progress toward goals, and identify opportunities to improve development performance.
Maintain accurate development records and support CRM management processes.
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure fundraising materials effectively communicate organizational priorities, impact, and future plans.
Manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining high-quality work in a fast-paced environment.
Participate in donor meetings and travel as needed.

Requirements

The ideal candidate will bring significant nonprofit fundraising experience, strong communication abilities, and a passion for mission-driven work. This position requires someone who can independently manage development initiatives while collaborating effectively with senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

5+ years of experience in nonprofit, political fundraising, advocacy, or media development roles.
At least 3 years of experience directly soliciting major gifts.
Proven experience managing major donor relationships and foundation fundraising initiatives.
Experience creating donor-facing materials such as proposals, presentations, decks, and strategic communications.
Experience supporting principal-level donor management and high-level fundraising efforts.
Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills with the ability to communicate organizational impact effectively.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities, deadlines, and projects.
Familiarity with Salesforce or similar CRM platforms.
Ability to work closely with executive leadership and provide strategic fundraising support.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment requiring flexibility and initiative.
Ability to build systems, processes, and workflows within a growing organization.
Strong alignment with the organization’s mission and commitment to equity, inclusion, and social impact.
Willingness and ability to travel, including occasional evening or weekend commitments.

Benefits

Competitive full-time salary of approximately $150,000 annually.
Fully remote work environment.
Opportunity to lead fundraising strategy for a growing mission-driven organization.
High level of ownership and influence over development initiatives.
Collaborative work environment with senior leadership and cross-functional teams.
Opportunity to build and improve fundraising systems and processes.
Meaningful work supporting advocacy, journalism, and education initiatives.
Professional growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding organization.

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process
to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice:
By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
